```plain
Based on recent literature reviews, there has been consistent support indicating that endoscopy remains the gold standard for both diagnosis and initial evaluation of patients suspected of having Zenker's diverticulum. While computed tomography (CT), magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), and upper gastrointestinal series can help rule out complications or comorbidities, they lack the precision necessary for pinpointing the location and characteristics of the diverticulum compared to endoscopy. Furthermore, endoscopy also facilitates biopsies if malignancy is suspected, reducing further invasiveness into the condition once diagnosed definitively under direct vision provided only by this methodological route.
